However, primary indications remain focused on increasing alveolar ridge height necessary for successful implant placement rather than directly addressing existing defects needing correction similar to gingival augmentation tasks covered by Options C & D. Therefore, although sharing some technical similarities particularly concerning tissue preservation principles, the main purpose diverges significantly toward structural enhancement versus corrective measures. Thus, I lean slightly towards maintaining agreement with earlier conclusions favoring Option D unless stronger evidence explicitly linking it to sinus augmentation emerges.
